# Feedback — Product Context

## Purpose

Feedback represents raw input from customers or testers.

It is the starting point of the product workflow.

---

## Core concept

Feedback = what people said

It is:

- unstructured
- repetitive
- noisy
- incomplete

It is NOT:

- analyzed
- structured
- validated

---

## Sources

Feedback may come from:

- public portal
- widget
- manual entry
- customer success
- future integrations (Slack, calls, etc.)

---

## What users should do here

Users should:

- review incoming feedback
- identify patterns
- detect repeated demand
- decide what should become a Request

---

## UX expectations

Feedback should feel like:

- an inbox
- an intake queue
- a demand stream

It should NOT feel like:

- a roadmap
- a planning tool
- a structured analysis view

---

## Actions

Users must be able to:

- read feedback
- filter feedback
- create a Request from feedback
- link feedback to existing Request

---

## Rules

- Feedback does not contain structured problems or solutions
- Feedback is not the place for decision-making
- Feedback must remain separate from Requests
